name: empty_range_when_equal
reason: When `lo == hi`, the range is empty — correct behavior but verifies the boundary case where the set contains no elements

name: range_contains_lo
code:
proof fn phi_2_range_contains_lo(lo: nat, hi: nat)
    requires
        lo < hi,
    ensures
        set_nat_range(lo, hi).contains(lo),
{
}
reason: The range always contains its lower bound when non-empty — a correct property verifying set membership at the boundary

name: range_excludes_hi
code:
proof fn phi_3_range_excludes_hi(lo: nat, hi: nat)
    requires
        lo < hi,
    ensures
        !set_nat_range(lo, hi).contains(hi),
{
}
reason: The range is half-open `[lo, hi)` and excludes `hi` — verifies the upper bound exclusion

name: singleton_range_len_1
reason: A range of width 1 has exactly one element — tests the minimal non-empty case

name: range_subset_monotone
reason: Narrowing the lower bound produces a subset — correct monotonicity property, but shows the spec allows arbitrary range nesting without additional constraints
